Members allocate the money independently to developers and take no role in protocol governance, positioning the effort as funding rather than direction as work on defenses like BIP 360 remains years from deployment.

title: Ramp Adds Stablecoin Accounts and Bill Pay on Stripe Stack
blurb: Ramp launched two stablecoin products on its $200B-annualized-volume corporate finance platform, running on Stripe's Bridge orchestration and Privy wallet infrastructure. Stablecoin Accounts hold USDC or USDT balances earning up to 3.25%, while Bill Pay sends stablecoins to vendors in 140-plus countries directly from a USD bank account, so customers without crypto never touch the tokens.

title: The Consensus Number of Untraceable Cryptocurrencies
blurb: This paper analyzes how sender-untraceable cryptocurrency designs affect distributed synchronization by placing two models in the consensus hierarchy. Classical schemes that append nullifiers and grow the ledger (LUAT) have a consensus number of 2 and are starvation-free, while constant-state schemes that consume and replace masking sets (CUAT) have a consensus number that grows quadratically with masking-set size and sacrifice fairness. The takeaway: the same privacy guarantee is paid for either in storage or in coordination cost.

title: Why vaults are the wrong structure for fixed-rate lending
blurb: The author argues that vaults are structurally wrong for fixed-rate lending because they hide who actually underwrites the debt. A fixed-rate loan carries specific duration and credit risk, but a vault depositor picks none of it, becoming counterparty to whatever loans the pool funds while still expecting instant withdrawals. The mismatch means duration risk and bad debt get socialized across everyone in the vault, rather than sitting with a real counterparty who accepted the terms and gets paid for them.

title: Movement Labs Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
blurb: Movement Labs, developer of the Movement blockchain, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, listing $100,000 to $500,000 in assets against more than $1M in liabilities and fewer than 1,000 creditors, with co-founder Rushi Manche and Anchorage Digital among the largest. The filing follows a MOVE token market-making scandal, an internal investigation, and Binance's ban of the related market-making account.

title: BitMEX to Shut Down Exchange on Sept. 23, Urges Withdrawals
blurb: BitMEX will permanently shut down its exchange on Sept. 23, telling users to close positions and withdraw funds ahead of the deadline after HDR Global Trading concluded a strategic review that followed an unsuccessful sale process begun in early 2025. The BMEX token fell roughly 91% over 24 hours to a circulating market cap near $540,000, and reduce-only risk limits take effect Aug. 26.

title: Strategy, BlackRock form Bitcoin Security Consortium to prepare for quantum computing threat
blurb: Strategy, BlackRock, Coinbase and six others formed the Bitcoin Security Consortium, pledging $15 million over three years to fund Bitcoin developers and security research, with post-quantum cryptography as the first focus. The group will direct capital independently and explicitly stay out of protocol governance, leaving development to Bitcoin's open-source community while coordinating day-to-day work through Brink's Mike Schmidt.

title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
blurb: The Clarity Act, the crypto industry's marquee market-structure bill, has stalled in the Senate over a Democratic push to bar the president and other officials from issuing or selling digital assets while in office. The provision targets Trump's crypto ventures and threatens to fracture the bipartisan support the bill needs to clear a floor vote.

title: Towards Automated Formal Verification of zkEVMs Using LLM-Guided Constraint Synthesis
blurb: VeriSynth is a framework that synthesizes executable Python/Z3 verification models from Rust zkEVM opcode handlers, using an LLM only as a formalization frontend while an SMT solver arbitrates correctness. The design targets bugs like incorrect gas accounting, where valid proofs can certify faulty states and silently defeat a rollup's cryptographic guarantees. On the first source-level zkEVM verification benchmark, it caught over 90% of bugs, beating direct LLM baselines and a production mutation-testing suite.

title: MoonPay now accepts Discover Network cards for crypto purchases and sales in US
blurb: MoonPay added Discover as its third supported US card network for crypto purchases and sales, alongside Visa and Mastercard.

title: How Do Stablecoins Maintain Their Peg?
blurb: Stablecoins hold their target price, usually one U.S. dollar, through two mechanisms: reserves or collateral that back each token's value, and arbitrage that pulls the market price back toward the peg when it drifts. This guide from The Block walks through both, covering fiat-collateralized designs like USDC, overcollateralized crypto-backed models like DAI, and the mint-and-redeem arbitrage loop that keeps traders correcting deviations.

title: Flow Traders pilots Lombard’s new bitcoin-backed credit strategy for stablecoin borrowing
blurb: Lombard Finance launched its Bitcoin Onchain Credit Strategy, with Flow Traders as the pilot partner deploying bitcoin as collateral to borrow stablecoins onchain. The strategy routes BTC-backed lending through a structured credit product, giving institutional desks a way to draw stablecoin liquidity against bitcoin holdings without selling the underlying asset.

title: Arbitrum protocol AFX Trade hit by $24 million bridge exploit: Blockaid
blurb: Arbitrum-based derivatives protocol AFX Trade lost roughly $24M in a bridge exploit, according to Blockaid, with the attacker moving the stolen funds cross-chain to Ethereum. PeckShield tracked the laundering route, showing the exploiter bridged the assets from Arbitrum and swapped them into 12,467 ETH.

title: New Verus-Ethereum bridge attack drains $7.5 million through flaw used in May: Blockaid
blurb: Attackers drained $7.54 million from the Verus-Ethereum bridge, exploiting the same vulnerability class used in a May attack, according to security firm Blockaid. The repeat exploit within two months marks the second breach of the bridge, raising questions about cross-chain bridge security and the durability of patches applied after the initial incident.

title: Bipartisan support ‘critical’ as Democrats push back on GOP crypto bill on ethics grounds
blurb: The latest draft of the Clarity Act, which would set a market-structure framework for digital assets, is drawing praise from the crypto industry while Democrats and banking groups push back on ethics and consumer-protection grounds. The bill's sponsors say bipartisan support is critical to passage, but the deepening partisan split signals a harder path through the Senate.

title: Clarity Act’s impact on Circle could be negative over the long term, Mizuho says
blurb: Mizuho analysts said the Clarity Act could hurt Circle's long-term economics by opening the door to competing dollar-stablecoin issuers and potentially allowing yield-bearing alternatives that erode USDC's reserve-interest revenue. The warning came Wednesday as House Republicans released updated text for the market-structure bill, moving it a step closer to becoming law.

title: Ostium to Reopen Trading July 23 After $23.8M Vault Exploit
blurb: Ostium, a perpetuals trading protocol on Arbitrum, will reopen trading on July 23, one week after an attacker compromised its off-chain price infrastructure and drained roughly 23.8M USDC from its liquidity provider vault. Trader collateral sat in an isolated contract and was untouched, and the protocol, which held about $37.8M in TVL, has paused new OLP deposits while it works with Mandiant and law enforcement on recovery.

title: KAIO Tokenizes Mubadala Capital Fund Across Base, Solana and Sui
blurb: KAIO launched tokenized access to a Mubadala Capital evergreen private-markets strategy simultaneously across Base, Solana, and Sui, citing roughly $75M in onchain value from traditional and digital-asset investors. Coinbase said it will add exposure to the offering, extending a sovereign-linked private-markets product to a listed US exchange, though the token contract addresses and Mubadala's own confirmation were not published alongside the launch.

title: Ripple’s RLUSD gets two boosts as transfer volume drops 25%
blurb: Ripple launched Ripple Mint, an automated platform for institutions to create, redeem, bridge, and track its RLUSD stablecoin, and made a strategic investment in compliance network Notabene to route the token through institutional payment rails. The moves come as RLUSD's monthly transfer volume fell 25% to $10.95 billion against a market cap near $1.5 billion, a sign the stablecoin is being held more than used.

title: Clarity Act expected to miss its window before Congress' summer break, leadership says
blurb: Senate Majority Leader John Thune said the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act will likely miss its August 7 target to pass before the summer recess, sharply reducing its odds of enactment in 2026. Thune hopes to at least begin floor debate before the break, but a contested working draft and looming midterm politics leave the market structure bill fighting for limited September floor time.

title: Tassat wants to help smaller banks tap the trillion-dollar stablecoin boom before Wall Street locks them out
blurb: Tassat, the fintech behind Signature Bank's former Signet payments network, launched Project NENYA, a reserve-management marketplace connecting regulated stablecoin issuers with regional and midsize US banks to allocate reserves across cash deposits and tokenized high-quality liquid assets. The platform targets smaller lenders that lack the compliance infrastructure to service issuers directly, with pilots slated for early 2027 as stablecoin reserves concentrate at a handful of large institutions.

title: The SEC settles with Coinbase over its missing Gary Gensler texts
blurb: The SEC agreed to pay $150,000 and produce its remaining records to settle a FOIA lawsuit brought on Coinbase's behalf over its Ethereum investigations, ending a two-year fight that exposed the deletion of Gary Gensler's texts and data wiped from 21 officials' phones. The case will be dismissed once final production is complete, closing one of the last legal overhangs from the SEC's prior crypto-enforcement era.

title: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Capital joins tokenization push as Coinbase takes stake in onchain fund
blurb: Mubadala Capital, the asset management arm of Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund, tokenized one of its private markets strategies using KAIO infrastructure, launching the fund on Base, Solana, and Sui with about $75M in onchain assets already. Coinbase is taking exposure to the fund on its own balance sheet, an early case of a listed crypto company holding a tokenized private markets product.

title: South Korea's oldest crypto exchange is now part of the $1 trillion Mirae Group family
blurb: Mirae Asset Consulting, an affiliate of the $1 trillion Mirae Asset Group, acquired a 97.15% controlling stake in Korbit, South Korea's oldest crypto exchange, marking the first time a traditional Korean financial group has taken control of a domestic exchange. The deal continues a wave of Asian TradFi conglomerates buying licensed exchanges, following SBI Group's $289M purchase of Japan's Bitbank and its majority stake in Singapore's Coinhako.

title: Uniswap pushes deeper into tokenized assets with permissioned trading pools
blurb: Uniswap Labs is rolling out Permissioned Pools, a framework built with Securitize, Superstate, and Dowgo that lets tokenized funds, equities, and other regulated assets trade through its automated market maker while restricting participation to approved investors. Issuers can enforce eligibility rules onchain without building separate trading infrastructure, extending Uniswap's AMM into an RWA market Citi projects could reach $5.5T in tokenized securities.

title: Arbitrum-based AFX Trade drained of $24 million after bridge keys compromised
blurb: AFX Trade, a decentralized perpetuals exchange on Arbitrum, was drained of $24.15 million in USDC after an attacker compromised the hot validator signing keys behind a bridge the protocol operates, then bridged the funds to Ethereum and swapped them for roughly 12,467 ETH. Arbitrum's native bridge was not breached, and Blockaid said the on-chain logic worked as designed, with five hot-validator signatures clearing the bridge's two-thirds quorum.

title: It’s time to pass the CLARITY Act
blurb: a16z's Chris Dixon argued Congress should pass the CLARITY Act to give blockchain networks the same regulatory framework the GENIUS Act gave stablecoins, citing a stablecoin market that has grown more than 50% year-over-year to roughly $315B and processed $100T in volume by Visa's count. He framed the bill as market structure legislation that would anchor tokenization and crypto infrastructure in US standards rather than ceding the ground to competing jurisdictions.

title: Marqeta partners with Zerohash for stablecoin-linked cards
blurb: Marqeta partnered with Zerohash to let its card programs embed stablecoin payments without rebuilding core systems or taking on new regulatory burden, making stablecoin-linked cards spendable at tens of millions of merchants. The move plugs into Marqeta's existing base of scaled clients but pits its distribution against Rain, a $1.95B Visa principal member whose Puerto Rico issuing keeps more interchange per swipe.

title: Wu Blockchain: Zilliqa Ledger flaw exposes private keys
blurb: Zilliqa disclosed a critical nonce-generation flaw in its Ledger app that lets private keys be recovered from public signatures after roughly five native transactions, with active exploitation observed on July 19 and all 2019-2026 versions affected. Native transactions are suspended and Upbit has flagged ZIL as a cautionary asset across its KRW and BTC markets, freezing deposits and withdrawals pending a fix.

title: Hacker Behind Fake 'Vladhood' Token Still Collecting Fees After Robinhood CEO's X Account Hack
blurb: A hacker compromised Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account to promote a fake VLAD memecoin on Robinhood Chain, part of a coordinated operation that launched the token hours earlier and drew over $22M in trading volume across roughly 85,000 swaps. Rather than pulling liquidity, the wallet repeatedly called Pons launchpad's fee-collection function, netting about 31.6 ETH and 72M VLAD worth roughly $247,000 combined.

title: How onchain analysts demix Tornado Cash transactions
blurb: This walkthrough breaks down how onchain analysts de-anonymize Tornado Cash withdrawals, using deposit-and-withdrawal timing correlation, reused relayers, gas-funding trails, and address-clustering heuristics to link mixed funds back to their source. The author illustrates each technique with real transaction traces, showing how the mixer's anonymity set collapses when users batch deposits, reuse wallets, or withdraw to previously linked addresses.

title: Melee Launches Parimutuel Market Maker for Solana Prediction Markets
blurb: Melee launched a parimutuel market maker for prediction markets on Solana, pooling all stakes on an outcome and paying winners proportionally from the losing side rather than quoting prices through an order book or constant-product curve. The design removes the need for dedicated liquidity providers, letting low-volume or long-tail markets settle without the thin books and slippage that typically plague on-chain prediction venues.

title: Morpho Midnight Analysis
blurb: Morpho launched Midnight, a fixed-term loan product that prices loans like bonds: lenders buy a credit unit at a discount that redeems at par at maturity, locking the rate rather than letting it reprice each block. The design targets institutional loan books already on Morpho, including Coinbase's $1.4B+ cbBTC-backed facility. Terms across 1, 3, and 6 months form a yield curve, though offer-based liquidity remains unproven at scale.

title: 24/7 financial rails: How BNY plans to eliminate the weekend lag in U.S. Treasuries
blurb: BNY, the world's largest custody bank, plans to enable 24/7 settlement of conventional and tokenized U.S. Treasuries by 2027, after an after-hours test cleared trades tied to Ripple's RLUSD and OpenEden's USDO reserves once Fedwire Securities had closed. The bank will begin testing tokenized Treasuries on a private blockchain by year-end, targeting the weekend and off-hours settlement gap that currently delays reserve adjustments for stablecoin issuers.

title: Circle partners with Kakao, Toss Bank to explore stablecoin payment rails in South Korea
blurb: Circle signed separate MOUs with Kakao Group and Toss Bank to explore stablecoin payment rails in South Korea, building on its April agreements with exchanges Upbit and Bithumb. The deals give Circle a foothold in Kakao's super-app ecosystem and Toss's internet-only bank as it pushes USDC, currently at $74.4B in supply, into Korean retail payments.

title: Uniswap Adds Permissioned Pools to Bring Regulated Assets to v4
blurb: Uniswap introduced Permissioned Pools, a v4 hook standard that enforces issuer-controlled allowlists onchain, verifying wallet approval at the protocol level before any swap or liquidity mint rather than at the frontend. Launch partners Superstate, Securitize, and Dowgo are using it to bring tokenized funds, equities, and securities to AMM liquidity, targeting a tokenized real-world asset market Uniswap pegs at $11T by 2030.

title: Sources: Stripe is in talks to acquire OpenRouter, which helps developers use AI models and could fetch ~$10B; PitchBook: OpenRouter was valued at $1.3B in May (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Stripe is in talks to acquire OpenRouter, a marketplace that routes developer traffic across AI models, in a deal that could reach ~$10B, according to sources cited by the Wall Street Journal. The price would be a steep markup on OpenRouter's $1.3B valuation from May, signaling Stripe wants to own AI-model billing and routing infrastructure alongside its core payments stack.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institution-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings, making the Tokyo-listed financial group a strategic investor in the US exchange. The raise funds expansion of EDX's clearing and settlement capabilities and follows its OCC application to charter EDX Trust, a proposed national trust bank for regulated custody and settlement.

title: Ant International raises $1.2B for global expansion
blurb: Ant International raised $1.2B to fund global expansion, deepening its push into cross-border payments, treasury management, and merchant acquiring outside China. The round positions the Ant Group affiliate to compete more directly with Stripe, Adyen, and Wise on international settlement rails, particularly across Southeast Asia and emerging markets where it already processes large wallet-to-wallet volumes.

title: Revolut confirms fresh secondary share sale, at reported $115BN valuation
blurb: Revolut confirmed a fresh secondary share sale valuing the digital bank at a reported $115B, letting employees cash out shares priced at $2,017 each. The mark is up from $75B last year and $45B in 2024, and comes as Revolut, now past 75M retail customers, pursues US and France banking licences after winning its UK charter.

title: Revolut hits $115bn valuation in employee share sale, reports say
blurb: Revolut reached a $115B valuation in a secondary share sale for employees, pricing each share at $2,017 and up from the $75B its November secondaries commanded. The jump moves Revolut toward its $200B target for a potential 2028 listing and would sharply expand CEO Nik Storonsky's stake under a valuation-linked incentive deal.

title: Coinbase calls AI payments its ‘most high-conviction bet’ as businesses begin accepting agent payments
blurb: Coinbase will let businesses accept payments from AI agents through Coinbase Payments and the x402 standard, which it framed as its "most high-conviction bet." The move positions Coinbase to capture agent-driven commerce, where autonomous bots settle transactions in stablecoins over crypto rails rather than card networks with their interchange and authorization overhead.

title: Ondo's Oasis Pro Markets Cleared to Offer Tokenized Stocks in US
blurb: Ondo Finance's broker-dealer subsidiary, Oasis Pro Markets, secured SEC and FINRA authorization to offer tokenized US equities, ETFs, and mutual funds to American investors, extending a tokenization business that previously sold tokenized stocks only offshore. The clearance supports omnibus account structures through existing broker-dealer and advisory channels, giving institutions, RIAs, and retirement accounts a compliant path to tokenized securities without a launch date yet.

title: 5 Interesting Learnings from Stripe at $6.8 Billion in Revenue: 33% Growth, 47% Free Cash Flow Margins, and a $53B Bid for PayPal
blurb: The Information reported that Stripe's 2025 revenue reached $6.8B, up roughly 33% year-over-year and its fastest growth since 2021, on $3.2B of free cash flow for a 47% FCF margin. Growth re-accelerated because Stripe takes a cut of AI-native customers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Cursor, and Lovable, inheriting their compounding revenue without carrying their acquisition costs.

title: Coinbase's corporate customers can now accept payments from AI agents
blurb: Coinbase Business users can now accept payments directly from AI agents this week, using the x402 protocol Coinbase incubated to let agents pay in USDC with no extra merchant setup. The rollout also ships a Coinbase Developer Platform x402 SDK and real-time agent order streaming in Coinbase for Agents, positioning the exchange as the settlement layer for agent-to-business commerce.

title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs Clarity Act despite banking industry's concerns over stablecoin rules
blurb: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon endorsed the CLARITY Act, calling it imperfect but necessary for creating a level playing field and regulatory certainty in digital assets. His backing splits Wall Street from JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon and other bank leaders, who oppose provisions letting crypto firms offer yield-bearing stablecoins that resemble bank deposits without equivalent oversight, as senators eye a Senate floor vote next week.

title: BitMEX, the exchange that invented perps, is shutting down
blurb: BitMEX will shut down operations on Sept. 23, 2026, ending an 11-year run for the exchange that invented the 100x-leverage perpetual swap and built the foundational plumbing for crypto derivatives trading. The wind-down follows years of ceding perps volume to nimbler centralized rivals and decentralized venues, as liquidity, market makers, and whales migrated to platforms with deeper books and fewer legal hangovers.

title: UK fintech funding cools to £1.1bn in H1 2026
blurb: UK fintech companies raised £1.1bn ($1.5bn) in H1 2026, down 35% from H2 2025, with a 45% collapse in late-stage funding to $830m accounting for almost the entire decline, per Tracxn. Seed and early-stage activity held firm or grew and megarounds continued at six deals above $100m, signaling caution at the scale-up stage rather than a broad retreat from the sector.

title: Polymarket to challenge France’s nationwide website block
blurb: Polymarket said it will challenge France's nationwide website block, arguing the order from the National Gambling Authority is disproportionate because it cuts off users who visit only for market probabilities, even though trading from France was disabled in November 2024. The regulator counters that the platform still promoted unauthorized gambling and cited loss, identity-check, and manipulation risks, making France the latest country to order an ISP-level block after Ukraine, Argentina, and Spain.

title: LayerZero, Keeta enable tokenized bank deposits across Ethereum, Solana and Base
blurb: LayerZero and Keeta partnered to enable native transfers of tokenized bank deposits across Ethereum, Solana, Base, and Keeta's own chain, using LayerZero's cross-chain messaging as the settlement layer. The integration lets banks issue deposit tokens that move directly between chains without wrapping or bridging, positioning tokenized deposits as a multi-chain alternative to stablecoins for institutional settlement.

title: Tokenized Stocks Hit Records Across Every Major Venue as Sector Reaches $2.3B
blurb: Tokenized stocks reached a record $2.3B market cap in mid-July, nearly doubling since March, with all-time highs across Ondo Finance, Backed Finance, and Robinhood Chain hitting the same week. Ondo leads with $955M in onchain equities after switching on 24/7 minting, adding voting rights, and partnering with Japan's SBI Group to tokenize Japanese stocks and settle in a yen stablecoin.

title: Ramp Adds Stablecoin Accounts and Bill Pay on Stripe Stack
blurb: Ramp launched two stablecoin products on its $200B-annualized-volume platform, running on Stripe's Bridge orchestration and Privy wallet infrastructure: stablecoins as a Bill Pay funding option and Stablecoin Accounts that hold, earn, and move USDC and USDT. Accounts pay up to 3.25% in rewards with no USD-to-stablecoin conversion fees, and let businesses pay vendors in 140-plus countries around the clock, though they exclude New York customers.

title: Tech Bonds Hit by Selloff as AI Debt Fears Race Through Markets
blurb: Bonds of the largest US tech companies sold off Thursday as investors reassessed the scale of debt financing the AI infrastructure boom, compounded by escalating Middle East conflict. The move signals credit markets are starting to price AI capital spending as a risk factor, raising borrowing costs for the hyperscalers and data-center operators whose buildouts increasingly depend on cheap debt.

title: Nubank to acquire Banco Porto Real de Investimentos, strengthen Brazil operation
blurb: Nubank agreed to acquire Banco Porto Real de Investimentos, a Rio de Janeiro wholesale-credit bank, adding its banking license to Nubank's existing Brazilian licenses pending Central Bank approval. The new license carries no additional capital or liquidity requirements and leaves service unchanged for Nubank's 115 million customers, deepening its position as Brazil's largest private financial institution by customer count.

title: Chinese Banks Break With Lending Benchmark in Pricing New Credit
blurb: Three of China's largest banks began pricing new loans against interbank rates rather than the central bank's loan prime rate, testing a benchmark that more directly reflects market supply and demand for funds. The move signals a gradual shift away from the PBOC's administered lending guidance toward market-set pricing, with implications for how credit is transmitted and how banks manage margins across the world's second-largest economy.

title: Chime launches in-app investing
blurb: Chime launched Chime Invest, letting users trade stocks and ETFs commission-free or hold an Atomic Invest-managed portfolio with no account minimums and management fees ranging from 0% to 0.25% by membership tier. The move deepens Chime's push to rebundle banking, investing, and lending in one app, keeping the roughly 50 monthly transactions per member inside its ecosystem rather than a third-party brokerage.

title: Amazon Business adds Affirm as its first BNPL option at checkout
blurb: Amazon Business added Affirm as its first buy-now-pay-later option at checkout, marking the launch of Affirm's new B2B pay-over-time product for sole proprietors. Eligible customers get an instant credit decision and can split eligible purchases across three to 48 months with no late or hidden fees, rolling out now and reaching all eligible sole proprietors by Black Friday.

title: £45m secondary share sale makes Moneybox a unicorn
blurb: Moneybox hit a roughly £800m valuation, up about 45% from its 2024 mark, after completing a £45m secondary share sale on the London Stock Exchange's Private Securities Market, with Apis Global Growth Fund III leading and Salica Fulcrum joining existing backers including Fidelity International. The deal raised no new capital, letting long-serving employees cash out shares to institutional investors, and follows 2025 revenue above £115m, a third consecutive profitable year, and £23bn in assets under administration.

title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
blurb: The Clarity Act, the crypto industry's priority market-structure bill, has stalled in the Senate over a Democratic push to bar the president and senior officials from issuing or profiting from crypto ventures. The provision has become the sticking point as the bill nears a floor vote, testing whether the industry can secure a federal regulatory framework without a Trump-focused conflict-of-interest carveout.

title: MoonPay now accepts Discover Network cards for crypto purchases and sales in US
blurb: MoonPay added Discover card support for US crypto buys and sells, making it the third major network alongside Visa and Mastercard.

title: Payments M&A spools up
blurb: Payments M&A activity is accelerating in 2026, with strategic acquirers and private-equity buyers moving on processors, gateways, and infrastructure providers after a multi-year lull in dealmaking. The pickup reflects steadier interest rates and pressure on subscale payments firms to consolidate, setting up more roll-ups as larger platforms buy distribution and rail coverage rather than build it.

title: Bipartisan support ‘critical’ as Democrats push back on GOP crypto bill on ethics grounds
blurb: The latest draft of the Clarity Act, the GOP-led crypto market structure bill, drew praise from the crypto industry but ran into pushback from Democrats citing ethics concerns and from bankers wary of its scope. With bipartisan support described as critical to passage, the divisions signal a harder path through the Senate and continued uncertainty over which regulator, the SEC or CFTC, ends up governing digital-asset markets.

title: Clarity Act’s impact on Circle could be negative over the long term, Mizuho says
blurb: Mizuho analysts said the Clarity Act could hurt Circle over the long term, arguing that clearer stablecoin rules will invite more competition into a market Circle's USDC currently dominates alongside Tether. The warning lands as Republicans released updated Clarity Act text on Wednesday, moving the crypto market-structure bill closer to law and setting up new interchange-style pressure on stablecoin issuers' yield economics.

title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
blurb: Upbound Group disclosed in an SEC filing that attackers used stolen customer data to open roughly $13 million in fraudulent Acima lease-to-own agreements during the second quarter, with Acima paying retailers for goods the fraudsters took without making payments. The company has since added authentication controls, fraud-detection mechanisms, and enhanced monitoring, underscoring how identity-based fraud, not ransomware, is the growing loss vector for lease-to-own and alternative-finance lenders.

title: Bank of America upgrades customer service employee tool
blurb: Bank of America added generative AI capabilities to EricaAssist, its internal tool for customer service employees, letting representatives resolve client inquiries faster by surfacing account information and next steps in real time. The upgrade extends the bank's Erica assistant, which has handled over 2.5 billion client interactions since 2018, from consumer-facing chat into agent-facing support workflows.

title: Tassat wants to help smaller banks tap the trillion-dollar stablecoin boom before Wall Street locks them out
blurb: Tassat, the fintech behind Signature Bank's former Signet payments network, launched Project NENYA, a stablecoin reserve management platform that connects regulated issuers with regional and midsize U.S. banks through a shared marketplace for allocating reserves across cash deposits and tokenized HQLA. Pilots begin in the first half of 2027, targeting smaller banks that lack the compliance infrastructure to service issuers as stablecoin reserves concentrate at a few specialist institutions post-GENIUS Act.

title: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Capital joins tokenization push as Coinbase takes stake in onchain fund
blurb: Mubadala Capital, the $600B asset-management arm of Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund, tokenized one of its private markets strategies for qualified investors using UAE-based KAIO, with the fund live on Base, Solana, and Sui and already holding roughly $75M in onchain assets. Coinbase is taking exposure on its own balance sheet, an early case of a public crypto firm investing directly in a tokenized private markets product.

title: ANNA Money makes two acquisitions to accelerate AI platform for SMEs
blurb: ANNA Money acquired UK Business Forums, a community platform for 160,000 UK small business owners, and Business Data Group, whose eFiling tool serves company-formation agents. The deals feed ANNA's plan to build a single AI-powered platform spanning formation, banking, bookkeeping, tax, and compliance, letting entrepreneurs move from incorporating a company to running it without switching tools.

title: Marqeta partners with Zerohash for stablecoin-linked cards
blurb: Marqeta partnered with Zerohash to let any of its customers embed stablecoin-linked cards into new or existing card programs without rebuilding core systems or taking on new regulatory burden. The move gives Marqeta's thousands of live programs a switch-on path to stablecoin spend, but its US model requires a sponsor bank that takes an interchange cut, unlike Puerto Rico-based rival Rain, which issues directly as a Visa principal member.

title: Intuit launches business credit card that syncs natively with QuickBooks
blurb: Intuit launched a business credit card that syncs transactions natively with QuickBooks, auto-categorizing spend and reconciling against the books in real time rather than through periodic feed imports. The card leans on Intuit's underwriting via QuickBooks financial data, targeting small businesses already inside the accounting platform and tightening its grip on the SMB money-movement stack.

title: Big banks' record Wall Street profits are increasingly tied to AI
blurb: The five largest Wall Street banks reported $114B in capital markets revenue for the first half of 2026, up 31.5% year-over-year, with stock trading tied to the AI boom driving more than half the gain. AI is fueling underwriting fees, equities trading, and wealth inflows from IPO employees, though executives including Jamie Dimon warned the trading haul is "close to as good as it gets.

title: Ramp launches AI token spend controls
blurb: Ramp launched AI Token Spend Management, a dashboard that lets finance teams track and cap AI token and subscription spend across Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor, and Gemini in one place. The tool ties usage to cost, flags savings and anomalies, and sets limits by team, project, or API key, addressing a category that has grown 20.7x across Ramp customers since June 2025.

title: Samsung launches Galaxy Card seven years after Apple Card
blurb: Samsung launched the Galaxy Card, a virtual-first credit card issued by Barclays on the Visa network, offering up to 5% cash back and managed inside Samsung Wallet, seven years after Apple Card debuted. The card mirrors Apple's model with tiered rewards, but Samsung's narrow services footprint means the headline 5% rate applies mostly to Samsung hardware purchases rather than everyday spending.

title: Recognise Bank makes first annual profit after restructure
blurb: Recognise Bank posted an £8.9m post-tax profit for the year ended 31 March 2026, its first ever, reversing a £5.3m loss after a restructuring that hit in-month profitability in May 2025, four months ahead of plan. The SME property lender grew its loan book 51.1% to £461.9m and deposits 18.8% to £575.5m, and took a £5m capital injection from top shareholder Parasol V27.

title: Augustus secures $180 million Series B
blurb: Augustus raised $180M in a Series B at a $1B valuation, led by Tiger Global with the founders of Nubank, Ramp, Circle, and Deel participating, to build a federally chartered bank giving international fintechs and banks direct access to USD accounts and payment rails. By pursuing a charter rather than renting one, Augustus is positioning to disintermediate the sponsor-bank layer that firms outside the US currently depend on for dollar clearing.
